title: [ExportVerilog] Inline duplicable unary op more aggressively
body:
For the following ir, 
```mlir
hw.module @Foo(%a: i2) -> (h:i1, j:i1) {
  %a0 = comb.extract %a from 0 : (i2) -> i1
  hw.output %a0, %a0 : i1, i1
}

```
The result is:
```sv
module Foo(	// bar.mlir:1:1
  input  [1:0] a,
  output       h, j);

  wire _a_0 = a[0];	// bar.mlir:2:9
  assign h = _a_0;	// bar.mlir:3:3
  assign j = _a_0;	// bar.mlir:3:3
endmodule
```
We can reduce number of temporary wires by inlining a[0].
```sv
assign h = a[0];
assign j = a[0];
```
